food_subgroup: Tea beverages — chilled / bottled jasmine tea

ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0EJW covers jasmine tea infusion as a category; chilled/bottled variant is not separately coded.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 300 ml (one bottle or cup), as typically served at Din Tai Fung Singapore and similar Chinese restaurants.

nutrient_source.title: Beverages, tea, ready-to-drink, jasmine, brewed — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy / Foundation Foods
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA FoodData Central data for brewed jasmine tea (unsweetened) used as the primary nutrient reference. The Pokka bottled jasmine tea served at Din Tai Fung Singapore is a lightly sweetened commercial product; sugar_g is adapted upward to approximately 5 g per 100 ml based on typical Pokka Jasmine Green Tea product label data (Pokka Pte Ltd Singapore, product label). All other macros remain consistent with brewed jasmine tea. Sodium is trace per USDA. Energy is recalculated from sugar content.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI value exists for bottled jasmine tea. Plain brewed tea and lightly sweetened tea beverages are classified as low GI (GI < 15) in the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care). The sugar content (~5 g per 100 ml) is predominantly sucrose; however, the low total carbohydrate load per serving means the glycemic load is negligible. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no significant glycemic carbohydrates beyond trace sugar; gi_category low per convention. glycemic_load_per_serving = 0.0 reflecting negligible GL at standard 300 ml serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (~2 mg per 100 ml; ~6 mg per 300 ml serving)
hypertension.guidance: Jasmine tea is very low in sodium and poses no meaningful blood pressure risk; individuals with hypertension may consume it freely, though they should avoid sweetened variants with added sodium or high sugar loads.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate content with approximately 15 g total sugar per 300 ml serving; low glycemic load
type2_diabetes.guidance: The lightly sweetened Pokka jasmine tea contains approximately 15 g of sugar per 300 ml serving, which contributes a modest carbohydrate load; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should account for this sugar intake within their daily carbohydrate budget and prefer unsweetened jasmine tea where possible.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero fat, zero saturated fat, zero cholesterol; jasmine tea polyphenols may confer mild cardioprotective benefit
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Jasmine tea contains no fat, saturated fat, or cholesterol, and the polyphenolic compounds in jasmine green tea have been associated with mild antioxidant and cardioprotective effects in observational studies; it is a heart-friendly beverage choice when consumed without excessive added sugar.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Jasmine tea (chilled) is a lightly sweetened, ready-to-drink tea beverage made by infusing green tea leaves with jasmine blossoms and chilling the resulting brew. In Singapore, it is commonly served as a bottled beverage — notably the Pokka Jasmine Green Tea brand — at Chinese restaurants including Din Tai Fung. The drink has a delicate floral aroma from the jasmine flowers, a light golden-green colour, and a mildly sweet, refreshing taste with subtle vegetal notes from the green tea base. It is essentially calorie-light (~20 kcal per 100 ml), containing approximately 5 g of sugar per 100 ml from added sucrose, with negligible protein, fat, and dietary fibre. The beverage is naturally caffeine-containing due to the green tea base. Nutritionally, it is a low-sodium, low-fat, low-calorie drink suitable for most dietary patterns. Its glycemic load per standard serving (300 ml) is low, making it broadly acceptable for individuals managing blood glucose, though those with Type 2 Diabetes should account for the ~15 g sugar per serving. The polyphenol content of jasmine green tea has been associated with antioxidant properties in the scientific literature. It is vegan, halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Pokka jasmine tea?
Is jasmine tea good for diabetics?
What is the sugar content of bottled jasmine tea?
Din Tai Fung jasmine tea calories

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central brewed jasmine tea data combined with Pokka product label information for sugar content. No exact HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN entry exists for this specific bottled product. The adaptation is well-reasoned and consistent with the product category, but an exact match to the Pokka Jasmine Tea product specification was not available from a primary approved database source.
notes: 1. protein_g is naturally zero — jasmine tea is an aqueous infusion containing no protein by nature. 2. fat_g is naturally zero — jasmine tea contains no fat by nature; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central brewed tea data. 3. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg are all naturally zero for this beverage. 4. dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as the tea is a filtered infusion with no fibre-containing solids. 5. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no significant glycemic carbohydrates beyond trace added sugar; gi_category low per convention; glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 reflecting negligible GL. 6. iron_mg is -1 as it is not reliably reported for this product category in the source database. 7. vitamin_c_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this product. 8. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 9. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported. 10. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported. 11. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported. 12. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported. 13. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported. 14. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported. 15. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported. 16. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported. 17. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported. 18. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported. 19. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported. 20. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported. 21. The food_name in the input ('冻顶茉莉花茶 Jasmine Pokka Tea') has been normalised to 'Jasmine Tea (Chilled)' as the canonical English name; the Chinese form and brand reference are preserved in aliases. 22. This record covers the Pokka Jasmine Tea bottled product as served at Din Tai Fung Marina Bay Link Mall, Singapore (field survey listing). 23. Multilingual names follow Singaporean dish rules: English (food_name), Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, Hindi.
